title: Compose gas validators with Firecat schema and define checkpoint convention
markdown:
## Goal

Register all gas validators into the Firecat schema so that a B3nd node running the Firecat protocol can process gas transactions alongside existing application messages. Also define the `confirmation://checkpoints` schema convention so that validators can record checkpoint hashes. This completes Phase 0: the gas system is schema-complete and testable with a MemoryClient.

## Background

The Firecat schema in `libs/firecat-protocol/mod.ts` is a `Schema` object (Record<string, ValidationFn>) that maps URI program keys to validators. Currently it includes:

- `mutable://open`, `mutable://inbox`, `immutable://inbox` -- open programs
- `mutable://accounts`, `immutable://accounts` -- auth-gated programs
- `immutable://open` -- write-once open
- `hash://sha256` -- content-addressed storage
- `immutable://balance`, `immutable://consumed`, `immutable://genesis` -- balance UTXO
- `consensus://record` -- consensus records
- `link://accounts`, `link://open` -- link programs

Gas validators need to be added:
- `gas://utxo` -- gas balance
- `gas://consumed` -- consumed gas markers
- `gas://genesis` -- gas genesis (test token minting)
- `gas://faucet` -- PoW faucet claims
- `gas://rates` -- fee rate configuration

Checkpoint convention: `confirmation://checkpoints/{validatorKey}/{sequence}` stores a hash of all messages processed in a checkpoint window. Value is `{ hash: string, from_sequence: number, to_sequence: number, timestamp: number }`. Write-once. This is a schema definition -- the runtime checkpoint logic comes in B2.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `libs/firecat-protocol/mod.ts` imports and registers all gas validators: `gas://utxo`, `gas://consumed`, `gas://genesis`, `gas://faucet`, `gas://rates`
- [ ] `confirmation://checkpoints` validator registered: value must be `{ hash: string, from_sequence: number, to_sequence: number, timestamp: number }`, write-once
- [ ] Gas validators are re-exported from `mod.ts` via `export * from "./gas-validators.ts"`
- [ ] Gas helpers and constants are re-exported via `export * from "./gas-helpers.ts"` and `export * from "./gas-constants.ts"`
- [ ] Integration test: a MemoryClient with the full Firecat schema can process a gas genesis claim, read the balance, spend gas to pay a fee, and record a checkpoint
- [ ] Integration test: gas transactions and regular Firecat transactions (mutable://open, hash://sha256) coexist without interference
- [ ] Integration test: a full end-to-end flow -- claim gas via faucet, pay gas fee on a consensus record
- [ ] Existing `validators_test.ts` tests continue to pass

## Implementation Guide

1. Read `libs/firecat-protocol/mod.ts` to understand how the schema is assembled
2. Import gas validators at the top of `mod.ts`:
   ```typescript
   import {
     gasUtxoValidator,
     gasConsumedValidator,
     gasGenesisValidator,
     gasFaucetValidator,
     gasRatesValidator,
   } from "./gas-validators.ts";
   ```
3. Add gas programs to the schema object:
   ```typescript
   "gas://utxo": gasUtxoValidator,
   "gas://consumed": gasConsumedValidator,
   "gas://genesis": gasGenesisValidator,
   "gas://faucet": gasFaucetValidator,
   "gas://rates": gasRatesValidator,
   ```
4. Implement and register `confirmationCheckpointValidator`:
   ```typescript
   "confirmation://checkpoints": async ({ uri, value, read }) => {
     // Write-once
     const existing = await read(uri);
     if (existing.success) {
       return { valid: false, error: "Checkpoint already exists (immutable)" };
     }
     // Validate shape
     if (typeof value !== "object" || value === null) {
       return { valid: false, error: "Checkpoint must be an object" };
     }
     const v = value as Record<string, unknown>;
     if (typeof v.hash !== "string" || typeof v.from_sequence !== "number" ||
         typeof v.to_sequence !== "number" || typeof v.timestamp !== "number") {
       return { valid: false, error: "Checkpoint must have hash, from_sequence, to_sequence, timestamp" };
     }
     return { valid: true };
   },
   ```
5. Add re-exports at the bottom of `mod.ts`:
   ```typescript
   export * from "./gas-validators.ts";
   export * from "./gas-helpers.ts";
   export * from "./gas-constants.ts";
   ```
6. Create `libs/firecat-protocol/gas-schema_test.ts` for integration tests:
   - Test: create MemoryClient with full schema, claim gas genesis, read balance
   - Test: pay gas fee on a mutable://open write (via envelope with gas inputs)
   - Test: claim faucet, then use faucet tokens to pay a fee
   - Test: write a confirmation checkpoint
   - Test: regular mutable://open writes still work without gas
   - Test: hash://sha256 content writes still work alongside gas

## Files to Create/Modify

- `libs/firecat-protocol/mod.ts` -- import and register gas validators + checkpoint validator, add re-exports
- `libs/firecat-protocol/gas-schema_test.ts` -- new integration test file

## Testing

```bash
# Gas-specific tests
deno test --allow-all libs/firecat-protocol/gas-validators_test.ts

# Integration tests
deno test --allow-all libs/firecat-protocol/gas-schema_test.ts

# Ensure existing tests unchanged
deno test --allow-all libs/firecat-protocol/validators_test.ts
```

## Dependencies

- **Depends on**: G3-B1-01 (gas UTXO), G3-B1-02 (fee validation), G3-B1-03 (faucet + rates)
- **Blocks**: G3-B2-01 (UTXO tracking), G3-B2-02 (fee calculation engine), G3-B2-03 (checkpoints)
tasksExcerpt:
- [ ] Implement `gas://utxo` program validators (creation, consumption, ownership verification)
- [ ] Implement UTXO existence and unspent checks
- [ ] Implement fee output validation (amount conservation: input amounts ≥ output amounts)
- [ ] Implement `gas://rates` program (static fee rates per program key)
- [ ] Implement `gas://faucet` program (one-time claim with PoW verification)
- [ ] Define `confirmation://checkpoints` schema and convention
- [ ] Create test token minting mechanism (self
